statement.en: Decision responsiveness and the legitimacy of public agencies data set
statement.summary: The data were collected in relation to the following goals: The research was designed to identify some of the key factors driving assessments of the legitimacy of decision-making agencies. The research was based on a set of (vignette-based) experiments that allowed the researchers to identify the effects on legitimacy of various aspects of decision-making responsiveness. Participants (N=438) were drawn from Prolific Academic, and completed an online survey fielded on Qualtrics. Assignment to treatment groups was randomised.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is an empirical dataset descriptor for a 2026-dated experimental study on agency legitimacy. The axiom is provisional because: (1) the dataset itself exists and is real, but (2) the causal claims about 'factors driving legitimacy assessments' remain subject to replication, effect-size debate, and methodological scrutiny; (3) vignette-based experimental findings are notoriously context-sensitive and validity-transferability is uncertain; (4) the work is dated future-relative to present, suggesting preprint/early-stage status. Classification as FLOWING reflects active validation-in-progress rather than settled truth or refutation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 権力の正当性は、市民の意志と行為の距離の逆数と、時間を通じた応答性の積分の和に直感的に比例する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 紫の市民意思から青の行政行為への双方向の流れが、緑の応答的選択によって結ばれ、琥珀色の信頼の曲線が両者を包含する。
